finding_results.notes: Replaced retrospective report with on-page escalation in scene-time: Tom grabs Elsie, boiling-water consequence lands immediately, Tom swings and impacts wall, and immediate physical aftermath (split knuckles, wrist marks, broken cups) is shown before scene progression.

Inserted two explicit Noreen interior beats inside the target span; each directly alters her next move (she physically wedges between Tom and Elsie, then strategically redirects Tom into Arthur's lantern task to prevent further strikes).

Replaced stock freeze shorthand with a concrete non-escalation behavior: Noreen de-escalates by physically easing Peter's hand away and controlling her own breath/body to avoid triggering immediate conflict.

Arthur's cited lines were shifted from polished moral framing to task-first electrical/household procedure, and an explicit operational limit was added (safe tonight, not tidy till morning; and conditional availability if he is in).

Noreen's confrontation now uses contraction-heavy, colloquial pressure cadence and grounds the argument in immediate house/lane specifics (back-gate bell, front-door routing, wall tapping, who crosses which step).

pass_summaries.summary: All packet findings for chapter_07 p1 structural/craft pass were resolved with targeted scene-level revisions focused on embodied causality, close-third interior pressure, and continuity-safe non-escalation behavior.

Both dialogue/idiolect findings were resolved with minimal collateral churn: Arthur now speaks in procedural under-pressure register with explicit practical limits, and Noreen's final exchange is colloquial, contraction-forward, and concretely anchored to physical surveillance dynamics in the house and lane.

All three prose/copyedit findings were resolved in-place with targeted line-level revisions that preserve chapter continuity, character register, and pressure while reducing template repetition and maintaining unsanitized specificity.
